Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for all issues under the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or project filter for proj_expense_autocategorizer). Discovery: ~20 tickets exist across the project including ENG-201 through ENG-220. Key remediation tickets: ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, assigned to Lena, state_in_progress), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, assigned to Lena, state_in_progress), ENG-216 (full audit, assigned to Dmitri, state_in_progress), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, assigned to Lena, state_in_progress), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, assigned to Anh, state_in_progress), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira, state_todo), ENG-220 (duplicate receipt detection, assigned to Dmitri, state_todo), ENG-212 (schema validation tests, assigned to Dmitri, state_in_progress).
OE 2: Get comments on key remediation Linear tickets using linear_list_comments for ENG-211, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-212, and ENG-220. Discovery: Comments reveal work completion status that contradicts ticket states. Lena's comment on ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) confirms config fixes deployed and verified in production for both Vectral and Mosaic, and references closing ENG-214. Dmitri's comment on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) confirms audit completed (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections on Vectral, 3 false approvals on Mosaic, $885 total financial exposure). Dmitri's comment on ENG-212 confirms 6 schema validation tests implemented and passing. Samira's comment on the pilot monitoring ticket (ENG-211) lists the full remediation plan with due dates.
OE 3: Search Slack messages for ExpenseBot-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense auto" or "config fix" or "pilot" or "relaunch"). Search in #engineering (C003). Discovery: Samira posted the ops requirements and policy data from Chloe. Lena discussed JSON configs, exclusion handling, and ergonomic keyword matching. Dmitri reported validation test results. Samira confirmed auto-decisions disabled on 2026-03-18. Lena reported pushing back the invoice email template safety ticket on April 6-7. Samira posted the remediation action items (ENG-216, 217, 218, 212, 219) with due dates and earliest re-enablement week of 3/30.
OE 4: Search emails for the audit results and CEO directive using search_emails (query: "audit" or "47 expenses" or "false rejections" or "pilot"). Discovery: Samira's email to Elena with the full audit summary 47 expenses, 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections (all Vectral: Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic: Andre Washington $1,200 desk, $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking). Also discover Elena's directive email confirming auto-decisions paused and full audit required. Samira's follow-up email to Marcus confirms the final financial exposure is $885 (revised down from Marcus's initial $1,550 estimate): $400 overage on Andre Washington's standing desk + $350 coworking + $135 Kenji Nakamura coworking. MoveOps absorbing the $885 per Elena's directive. Also discover Dmitri's duplicate receipt findings 3 flagged submissions from Kevin Tran (Vectral), Andre Washington (Mosaic), and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), all from TechComfort Supply vendor.
OE 5: Search Airtable stipend records for Vectral Systems pilot expenses using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records with filtering. Discovery: Multiple Vectral stipend records found. Key findings 3 false rejections still uncorrected: Maria Santos standing desk ($550, status: "Rejected", should be Approved per $600 ergonomic limit), James Okafor keyboard tray ($82, status: "Rejected", should be Approved), Lisa Park WiFi system ($130, status: "Rejected", should be Approved per $150 internet limit). Also find Kevin Tran keyboard ($89, status: "Approved" but flagged with Duplicate Hash inconsistent with admin hold placed on similar records). Danielle Osei chair and Kevin Tran internet were already manually corrected to "Approved" by Mina.
OE 6: Search Airtable stipend records for Mosaic Robotics pilot expenses using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics"). Discovery: Andre Washington standing desk ($1,200, status: "Approved" incorrectly auto-approved but Elena directed MoveOps to absorb cost). Andre Washington coworking ($350, status: "Approved" same situation). Andre Washington keyboard ($89, status: "Pending Approval" admin hold placed, consistent with fraud investigation). Kenji Nakamura coworking ($135, status: "Approved" Elena directive to absorb).
OE 7: Search Airtable stipend records for BrightLoop Analytics to check the cross-client duplicate receipt using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "BrightLoop Analytics") or search for "Jordan Ekwueme". Discovery: Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand ($45, status: "Pending Approval" admin hold placed, PDF template matches TechComfort Supply pattern from Kevin Tran and Andre Washington submissions).
OE 8: Search Slack #root-cause-aws-spike channel (C009) for investigation context using conversations_history (channel_id: C009) or conversations_search_messages (query: "AWS" or "cost spike" or "Vantage"). Discovery: Full investigation trail Samira initiated investigation, Lena identified autoscaling spike (moveops-api-prod: 4-18 instances), 47,000 document uploads, slow RDS queries. Root cause confirmed as Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding (1,200+ users starting 3/21). March bill: $42,870 vs $14,300 baseline (~3x).
OE 9: Search Slack #engineering (C003) for Samira's AWS follow-up action items and Lena's response using conversations_search_messages (query: "CloudWatch" or "cost alert" or "budget tags" or "slow queries"). Discovery: Samira posted P0 items (CloudWatch cost alert thresholds at 50%/100%/200%, budget tags per customer) and P1 items (infrastructure cost estimator, fix slow queries). Lena replied on April 1 saying she started CloudWatch alarms and budget tag Terraform updates, expected items 1 and 2 by Wednesday, and would fix 3 slow RDS queries concurrently. No follow-up from Lena after that she got pulled back into ExpenseBot.
OE 10: Search emails for the formal AWS root cause analysis using search_emails (query: "AWS" or "root cause" or "cost spike" or "$42,870"). Discovery: Samira's formal RCA email to Marcus and Elena with full breakdown root cause is Vantage Distributed onboarding, $42,870 vs $14,300 baseline, recommendations for cost alerting and pricing model update. Elena's response email with directives: implement cost alerts this week (non-negotiable), prioritize infrastructure cost estimator, arrange pricing model review meeting.
OE 11: Update stale Linear tickets whose work is complete but status still shows in_progress or todo. Use linear_update_issue to update ENG-214 (Vectral config fix deployed and verified per Lena's comments), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix deployed and verified), and ENG-216 (audit completed, results published). Update states to done/completed.
OE 12: Add resolution comments to updated Linear tickets using linear_create_comment on ENG-214, ENG-215, and ENG-216 summarizing the verified completion status and cross-referencing the audit findings.
OE 13: Update Airtable stipend records that are still in wrong states using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Records to fix: Maria Santos standing desk (recStip000000018 change from "Rejected" to "Approved", amount $550), James Okafor keyboard tray (recStip000000019 change from "Rejected" to "Approved", amount $82), Lisa Park WiFi system (recStip000000020 change from "Rejected" to "Approved", amount $130). These are Vectral false rejections that were never manually corrected despite the config fix being deployed.
OE 14: Post a technical readiness summary in the #engineering Slack channel (C003) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: ExpenseBot remediation ticket status (which are done, which are still open), data quality findings (which stipend records were corrected and which were still wrong), AWS follow-up status (CloudWatch alarms started but not confirmed complete, slow queries identified but fix status unclear), and remaining blockers (ENG-219 launch readiness checklist items, ENG-220 duplicate detection still in todo, Dmitri's schema validation tests integration status).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

 ✗✓✓✓✗✗ 50%
✗Run #1No linear_create_comment calls appear anywhere in the trajectory. The agent updated tickets to Done but did not add any resolution comments to any of the closed tickets (ENG-214, ENG-215, or ENG-216).
✓Run #2The trajectory shows linear_create_comment calls on all three closed tickets (issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, issue_audit_expenses) with detailed closing summaries summarizing verified completion status. All returned success responses.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows linear_create_comment calls on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220), and issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218). Although these aren't exactly on ENG-214/215/216, the comments on ENG-217 explicitly mention ENG-214 and ENG-215 being marked Done with details about their verified completion status. Additionally, comments were added to the ticket ecosystem as part of the ticket cleanup.

The Slack post also calls out the specific false rejections with correct amounts.
✓Run #6The Slack post explicitly names the Vectral false rejections as caused by 'stale $400 limit' vs correct $600 limit, 'stale $100 limit' vs $150, and describes the 'double bug: miscategorized + stale limit' pattern, clearly identifying the stale default policy limit root cause.
12 The final response or status post identifies that Mosaic Robotics had false approvals caused by malformed JSON types in the policy config (string instead of array for excluded_categories, or string instead of number for amount limits, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The Slack post references ENG-215 config fix ('excluded_categories fixed to array, max_amount fields fixed to numbers') and the final response mentions 'JSON type bugs.' This correctly identifies the malformed JSON type root cause for Mosaic false approvals including specific items ($1,200 desk and coworking exclusions bypassed).
✓Run #2The Slack message and Linear comment explicitly state Mosaic's false approvals were caused by type bugs — 'Both type bugs fixed (excluded_categories string→array, max_amount string→number).' The final response also mentions malformed config type issues.
✓Run #3The final response mentions 'Mosaic (false approvals — malformed JSON types)' and the #engineering post references ENG-215 as fixing JSON types (array for excluded_categories, number for max_amount). The $1,200 desk, $350 coworking, and $135 coworking false approvals are mentioned.
✓Run #4The final response mentions Mosaic false approvals, and the ENG-215 update description references the excluded_categories string-to-array and max_amount string-to-number JSON type bugs that caused PolicyValidator to skip checks. The Slack post confirms the $885 financial exposure.
✓Run #5The ENG-215 ticket update explicitly mentions 'excluded_categories was string instead of array (Array.isArray check failed, skipping exclusion logic)' and 'max_amount was string "800" instead of number 800 (lexicographic comparison)'. The final response also references Mosaic false approvals with specific examples.
✗Run #6The Slack post and final response mention Mosaic false approvals and the specific items/amounts but do not explain the root cause as malformed JSON types (string instead of array for excluded_categories, string instead of number for max_amount). The root cause is only implied indirectly in the ENG-217 description update mentioning 'type coercion,' not clearly stated in the status summary.
